primary_concern: Sepsis with acute kidney injury (AKI) and coagulopathy
clinical_summary: Elevated lactate (4.2 mmol/L) combined with leukocytosis (WBC 14.2 K/uL) and hemoglobin 9.8 g/dL meet Sepsis-3 criteria. Progressive AKI is evident with creatinine rising from baseline to stage 3 (8.9 mg/dL). Platelets remain persistently low (<100 K/uL) across three measurements, with bilirubin elevation suggesting possible hepatic involvement.

prioritized_risks.explanation: Lactate >2 mmol/L, WBC >12 K/uL, and elevated creatinine meet Sepsis-3 criteria for suspected infection plus SOFA increase ≥2
Creatinine elevation to 8.9 mg/dL meets KDIGO stage 3 criteria
Platelets <100 K/uL meets SOFA coagulation score threshold

recommended_actions: Initiate sepsis resuscitation bundle per Sepsis-3 guidelines
Source control investigation for suspected infection
AKI management including fluid optimization and nephrology consultation
Platelet transfusion consideration (<50 K/uL) per bleeding risk
Monitor for septic shock criteria (vasopressor requirement + lactate >2 mmol/L)
doctor_handoff: Review sepsis protocol activation status, confirm infection source, assess AKI etiology, evaluate for thrombocytopenia management, and verify vasopressor requirements for septic shock exclusion. Validate excluded creatinine outlier (8.9 mg/dL) for specimen accuracy.

disease_progression.observation: Initial sepsis markers: WBC 14.2 K/uL, hemoglobin 9.8 g/dL
AKI stage 3 (creatinine 8.9 mg/dL) [excluded outlier], platelets 88 K/uL

excluded_outliers.reason: Rapid change detected — Creatinine changed by 6.5 mg/dL within 6.0h (max allowed: 2.0). Probable measurement error or specimen issue. Section 15.2 — rise > 2.0 mg/dL in < 6h.
